items.q: What does 'mes parents' mean in French?
items.correct: My parents (mother and father specifically) - but 'parents' can also mean relatives more broadly.
items.misconception: It always means all my relatives.
items.why: Students think French 'parents' = English 'relatives' because in some contexts it does. Usually 'mes parents' = mother+father; 'mes proches' or 'ma famille' = relatives more broadly. Context matters.

items.q: When meeting a teacher or stranger for the first time, do you use 'tu' or 'vous'?
items.misconception: Tu, because it's singular and you're talking to one person.
items.random_wrong: Either is fine in modern French.
items.why: Students see 'vous' as plural only. In French, 'vous' is BOTH plural AND the formal singular. Using 'tu' with a stranger or authority figure is rude. Wait to be invited to 'tutoyer'.

items.q: How do you say 'I play the piano' vs 'I play football'?
items.correct: Je joue DU piano (instrument) vs je joue AU football (sport).
items.misconception: Same preposition for both - 'je joue piano', 'je joue football'.
items.random_wrong: Je joue le piano, je joue le football.
items.why: Students drop or confuse prepositions. The rule: jouer DE + instrument (de, du, de la); jouer À + sport/game (à, au, aux). Easy way to lose marks in speaking exams about hobbies.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my brother'?
items.correct: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.
items.misconception: Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.
items.random_wrong: Je entends bien avec mon frère.
items.why: Correct answer: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.. “Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which form of 'you' should you use when addressing your French teacher?
items.why: It's vous. “tu” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many forms does the French adjective 'beau' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur, masc before vowel 'bel')?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 forms. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
